channel.title: NPR Programs: Morning Edition
channel.description: Morning Edition takes listeners around the country and the world with multi-faceted stories and commentaries every weekday. Hosts Steve Inskeep, Leila Fadel, Michel Martin and A Martínez bring you the latest breaking news and features to prepare you for the day.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Key takeaways from Trump's China trip
item.description: President Trump returns to the U.S. after wrapping up his whirlwind trip to China.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump returns to the U.S. after wrapping up his whirlwind trip to China.

(Image credit: Evan Vucci)
item.dc:creator: Tamara Keith

item.title: China economist shares takeaways from the Trump-Xi summit
item.description: Steve Inskeep speaks with Chinese economist Keyu Jin about what came out of the Trump-Xi summit.
item.content:encoded: Steve Inskeep speaks with Chinese economist Keyu Jin about what came out of the Trump-Xi summit.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: Israel says Netanyahu secretly visited the UAE. The UAE denies it happened
item.description: The UAE's drawn closer to Israel since the Iran war, but a squabble over a secret visit by Israel's leader to the Arab state lays bare how sensitive those ties are
item.content:encoded: The UAE's drawn closer to Israel since the Iran war, but a squabble over a secret visit by Israel's leader to the Arab state lays bare how sensitive those ties are
item.dc:creator: Aya Batrawy

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: Trump returns to U.S. after trip to China, Supreme Court decides to maintain abortion pill access, U.K. prime minister faces challenges from his own party.
item.content:encoded: Trump returns to U.S. after trip to China, Supreme Court decides to maintain abortion pill access, U.K. prime minister faces challenges from his own party.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: How the relationship between the U.S. and China has changed under Trump
item.description: NPR's Steve Inskeep talks with Chinese scholar Da Wei about the history of the U.S.-China relationship and how it has changed under President Trump.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Steve Inskeep talks with Chinese scholar Da Wei about the history of the U.S.-China relationship and how it has changed under President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: Reps. Brian Fitzpatrick and Tom Suozzi on rising issue of gerrymandering
item.description: NPR's Michel Martin talks to Rep. Brian Fitzpatrick, R-Penn., and Rep. Tom Suozzi, D-N.Y., about what they believe is the nation's current greatest problem: gerrymandering.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Michel Martin talks to Rep. Brian Fitzpatrick, R-Penn., and Rep. Tom Suozzi, D-N.Y., about what they believe is the nation's current greatest problem: gerrymandering.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: 'Pocket gardens' give native plants a place to thrive in cities
item.description: "Pocket gardens" of native plant species are becoming more common in urban areas. We tag along with a volunteer tending to tiny gardens in a Washington, D.C., neighborhood.
item.content:encoded: "Pocket gardens" of native plant species are becoming more common in urban areas. We tag along with a volunteer tending to tiny gardens in a Washington, D.C., neighborhood.
item.dc:creator: Julie Depenbrock

item.title: From proposal to print: How books make it to store shelves
item.description: Our Planet Money team has gotten an inside peek at the publishing industry as they follow the journey of their book. They explain how economic forces shape what choices a bookstore makes.
item.content:encoded: Our Planet Money team has gotten an inside peek at the publishing industry as they follow the journey of their book. They explain how economic forces shape what choices a bookstore makes.
item.dc:creator: Alexi Horowitz-Ghazi

item.title: At this electronics market in China, buyers can find parts for just about everything
item.description: NPR's Steve Inskeep visits a massive electronics market in Shenzhen, China, where buyers can source parts for just about anything.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Steve Inskeep visits a massive electronics market in Shenzhen, China, where buyers can source parts for just about anything.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: Multigenerational households are becoming more common in the U.S.
item.description: Children, parents and grandparents all living together are becoming more common in the U.S. Multigenerational households also cause houses themselves to change.
item.content:encoded: Children, parents and grandparents all living together are becoming more common in the U.S. Multigenerational households also cause houses themselves to change.
item.dc:creator: Stephan Bisaha
